title: Trump’s $1.8 Billion Fund Tests Constitutional Limits
description: The deal the president reached with his own subordinates relies on a mechanism created by Congress that legal experts had warned was subject to manipulation.
author: Adam Liptak

title: Castro Indictment Is Trump’s Latest Move Against Cuba
description: For President Trump, the interest in Cuba is not new.
author: Tyler Pager

title: Trump Gets His Payback, but It Comes at a Cost in Congress
description: Republican senators are angry the president is working to unseat their colleagues. But he is also creating more free agents in his own party in Congress willing to defy him.
author: Carl Hulse

title: House Passes Housing Bill, Uniting on a Measure to Bring Down Costs
description: The legislation, which had been stalled amid Republican divisions, passed overwhelmingly, signaling an eagerness in both parties to address affordability in an election year.
author: Ronda Kaysen

title: Cuba is out of gas, prices are soaring and electricity is scarce.
author: Frances Robles

title: Prosecutor Is Charged With Stealing Sealed Report on Trump Documents Case
description: The Justice Department accused Carmen Lineberger of emailing records related to the special counsel’s investigation to a personal account.
author: Chris Cameron

title: Rubio Moves Closer to Decades-Long Goal of Transforming Cuba
description: The secretary of state is the son of immigrants who left Cuba before the Castro revolution to look for economic opportunities. Yet, he pursues change with the zeal of a political exile.
author: Edward Wong

title: How Rubio Is Driving the U.S. Pressure Campaign on Cuba
description: Our diplomatic correspondent Michael Crowley explains how Secretary of State Marco Rubio, who has long lobbied for an end to the regime in Cuba, is ramping up pressure on the island.
author: Michael Crowley, Nikolay Nikolov, Alexandra Ostasiewicz, Jon Miller and Whitney Shefte

title: Justice Dept. Charges Raúl Castro as Trump Escalates Pressure Campaign Against Cuba
description: The indictment was an extraordinary escalation of the Trump administration’s multifaceted pressure campaign against Cuba’s Communist government.
author: Alan Feuer, Frances Robles and David C. Adams

title: 1996 Downing of Planes by Cuba Followed Months of Diplomatic Wrangling
description: Cuban government and the Clinton administration held several meetings discussing the volunteer group’s flights.
author: Frances Robles

title: Raúl Castro Has Guided Cuba for Decades
description: Raúl Castro is 94 and no longer holds any official title, but he still wields enormous power, experts say, particularly over the military.
author: David C. Adams

title: All the Republicans Trump Has Taken Down in Midterm Primaries
description: The president has spent a great deal of political capital this spring trying to unseat fellow Republicans he sees as disloyal, and he has succeeded.
author: Jennifer Medina

title: Minnesota Becomes First State to Ban Prediction Markets
description: Like at least other 14 states, Minnesota has wrestled with how to regulate the popular prediction market sites. Federal officials sued the state over its new law.
author: Pooja Salhotra

title: Rubio, in Video, Urges Cubans to Align With Trump Administration
description: In a rare direct address, Secretary of State Marco Rubio blamed the former leader Raúl Castro for the country’s longstanding electricity and resource shortages.
author: Ali Watkins

title: Harvard Caps A’s as Selective Colleges Attack Grade Inflation
description: Faculty members overwhelmingly approved a limit on the number of top grades they can give to about a fifth of their undergraduate classes.
author: Mark Arsenault

title: Jan. 6 Police Officers Sue to Block Trump’s Payout Fund
description: The two officers accused the administration of creating a “slush fund” to reward rioters and groups that committed violence on behalf of President Trump.
author: Luke Broadwater

title: San Diego Mosque Shooting Follows Alarm About Rising Islamophobia
description: Muslims pointed to a rise in overt hatred online, political attacks and harassment in the months before the killing of three people outside an Islamic center.
author: Shaila Dewan and Jill Cowan

title: Barney Frank, Gay Pioneer and Liberal Stalwart in Congress, Dies at 86
description: Often voted the “brainiest,” “funniest” and “most eloquent” member of the House, he was also the first to come out voluntarily and helped normalize being openly gay in public office.
author: Katharine Q. Seelye

title: Massachusetts Police Will Pause Recruit Training to Fix Academy’s Flaws
description: State police officials say they need time to carry out recommendations made by an outside team after a trainee died in a police academy boxing match.
author: Jenna Russell

title: He Was Jailed Over a Charlie Kirk Post. The Sheriff Now Owes Him $835,000.
description: Larry Bushart sued a Tennessee sheriff who claimed he wanted to incite hysteria with a post after Mr. Kirk’s killing and jailed him for 37 days.
author: Rick Rojas
